trajectory.description: Observation: Starting fresh with no files. Analysis: This is a domain generalization task (train on L38, L43, L46, test on L100) with 10 wildlife classes. Key challenges: (1) domain shift, (2) class imbalance (raccoon ~52%, opossum ~20%). Strategy: Use DINOv2 ViT-B/14 as backbone (strong features), add a classifier head, train with class-weighted loss to handle imbalance. Start with a simple baseline to validate pipeline and measure iteration time. Decision: Write model.py, train.py, predict.py, then train for 5 epochs to get baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: Val_ba=0.8422 on source domains but test balanced_accuracy=0.6199 on L100. Large gap indicates domain shift. Weak classes: bird (0.296), squirrel (0.209), cat (0.560), rabbit (0.500). Analysis: The model overfits to source domain features. Need better domain generalization. Key issues: (1) Only 5 epochs trained; (2) Need stronger augmentation; (3) Class imbalance - focal loss; (4) Consider using both CLS token and mean pooling. Decision: Train longer (20 epochs) with stronger augmentation, focal loss, larger resolution, and label smoothing. Also use cosine LR with warmup.

trajectory.description: Observation: 20 epochs with focal loss + strong augmentation gave val_ba=0.8620 on source domains. Previous 5-epoch model got test=0.6199. Need to submit current model to measure improvement. The train/val loss gap (0.13 vs 0.82) shows overfitting to source domains. Analysis: The model needs better domain generalization. Key strategies: (1) Submit current checkpoint to measure progress, (2) Try CLIP backbone which has better zero-shot generalization, (3) Use backbone fine-tuning with lower LR, (4) More aggressive TTA. Decision: Submit current best checkpoint, then try CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone which was pre-trained on diverse internet images and may generalize better to new camera trap locations.

trajectory.description: Observation: 20-epoch model with focal loss got test=0.4563 vs 5-epoch model's 0.6199. More training = worse generalization. The model is severely overfitting to source domains. Analysis: Need to reduce overfitting. Strategy: (1) Use CLIP ViT-B/16 which has better zero-shot generalization, (2) Freeze most of backbone, only fine-tune last 2 transformer blocks + head, (3) Use simple weighted CE with label smoothing instead of focal loss, (4) Use weighted random sampler for class balance, (5) Lower LR. The 5-epoch model was better because less fine-tuning preserved generalizable features. Decision: Rewrite model.py to use CLIP, update train.py with frozen backbone strategy.

Decision: Rewrite to use DINOv2 with frozen backbone, train only head for 15 epochs, then optionally unfreeze last 2 blocks.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best test score is 0.6199 from DINOv2 5-epoch full fine-tuning. All other approaches (more epochs, frozen, CLIP, partial fine-tuning) are worse. Analysis: The sweet spot is minimal fine-tuning. The weak classes are squirrel (0.009-0.139), bobcat (0.175-0.456), bird (0.296-0.685). Strategy: Go back to original approach but improve it with: (1) label smoothing to prevent overconfidence, (2) mixup augmentation for better generalization, (3) use both CLS + mean patch tokens, (4) more aggressive TTA in predict.py, (5) train exactly 5 epochs. Also try a completely different angle: use the model's uncertainty to boost rare class predictions.
